positioning_facts: Local father-son owned and operated pest control company.
Specializes in treating scorpions and termites.
Mission is to bring peace of mind by protecting little ones from scorpions and other pests.
If an issue happens between regular pest services, the company says it is willing to come back for free.
Open Saturdays for customers who need a bug exterminator.

origin_story.summary: Owner Nelson Richardson started Richardson Pest Management after his 2-year-old daughter was stung by a scorpion, had a severe reaction, and had to be taken to the hospital. The site frames the company around protecting families from that experience.

process_facts.copy_basis: A technician comes to the property, discusses the issues, and aligns on the plan because each home is unique.
General pest treatments include exterior wall void flush, cinder-block wall void flush, and spraying vegetation bases. Interior treatments are available at no extra charge.
Pest control is a process, not an event. The site recommends treatment every other month to keep issues from resurfacing.

safety_facts: Products are described as safe for children, pets, and the environment.
The site names the products as pyrethrins and says they are organically derived.
The site says products are labeled for schools, animal shelters, and hospitals.
Once products have dried, normal activity may resume.

visual_facts.current_site_pattern: Light Weebly-style layout with low hierarchy, modal interruption, and important trust facts buried deep on the page.

non_negotiables: Use real Richardson logo and real Richardson imagery.
Do not invent review counts, years in business, guarantees, certifications, or awards.
Keep the founder story and family safety mission visible above or near the first scroll.
Make scorpion and termite specialization obvious without making the site feel gimmicky.
Preserve the phone CTA and East Valley location specificity.
